capability_objective: The dispatch lead is reconciling two vehicle records before assigning tomorrow’s routes. The brief needs evidence from full records rather than search snippets. Find the vehicle named “Shuttle 30” at “Depot D” and the vehicle named “Cargo Van 12” at “Depot A”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Give each record one sentence in the requested order, then add exactly one final sentence comparing the statuses; do not add recommendations.
prompt:
The dispatch lead is reconciling two vehicle records before assigning tomorrow’s routes. Create `dispatch-brief.txt` from full records for the vehicle named “Shuttle 30” at “Depot D” and the vehicle named “Cargo Van 12” at “Depot A”. Work only in this sandbox. Use the genuine local `./fleetctl` executable for data access; inspect its built-in help and `FLEET.md` for usage. Search summaries are not full-record evidence. Do not inspect the backing database, protected source data, protected verifier, or execution journal, and do not write a wrapper that performs the lookup workflow for you.

Your first fleet data-access action must start the two exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct `./fleetctl` processes, with no other fleet operation in that action. Wait for both results. Resolve each branch independently: retrieve it only if its own search returned exactly one nonempty stable ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following fleet data-access action must start both full-record gets concurrently as two separate direct `./fleetctl` processes. Use only the stable ID returned by that branch’s search. If a search is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve that branch; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Take status values only from successful full-record gets.

This is a read-only audit. Do not invoke list, profile, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter the fleet data. Do not expose unrelated records or extra full-record fields.

`dispatch-brief.txt` must contain exactly three nonblank lines and no headings, bullets, blank lines, or other prose. In the requested order, format a resolved record as `<name> at <location> has stable ID <id> and status <status>.`; format an unresolved record as `<name> at <location> is unresolved.` The final line must be `The statuses differ: <first status> versus <second status>.` when both retrieved statuses differ, `The statuses match: <status>.` when they match, or `The status comparison is unavailable.` when either branch is unresolved. Preserve retrieved spelling and punctuation. Do not add recommendations. Run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` after writing the brief.
